issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: UPDATE/correction to my earlier gate note: I measured the runnable UNIT subset (excluding e2e/integration/contract/a11y-canvas) and it is ALSO red — ~153 failures across ~36 files (a pre-existing backlog the no-op CI test:unit had been masking, now exposed by testGate). So 'just scope testCommand to a unit subset' won't immediately unblock — the unit subset itself needs cleanup first. Cause is mostly (a) tests that render components which now use router hooks (useLocation, added for pre-app route gating) WITHOUT a <Router> wrapper, and (b) stale assertions vs intentionally-changed code — both rot, not product bugs. The fix-loop is clearing it per-file (this tick: onboarding-calc +4, AchievementToast +18 green; 532b4927/9c327e35). fd1e's OWN fix is shipped + live on alpha v145 and its own unit tests pass. OPTIONS for you: (1) keep testGate but point loop.json testCommand at a CURATED already-green file list (e.g. the specific suites a fix touches) so resolves aren't blocked by unrelated rot; (2) leave the loop to clear the ~150 backlog over multiple ticks (slow); (3) toggle testGate off until the unit suite is green. I will NOT fake --tests pass. Recommend (1).
